subject: left-handed people
one_liner: Enterprise messaging mirrored right-to-left for the 10% of your workforce IT has been ignoring since the invention of the mouse.
brand_voice_notes: Grievance as enterprise compliance. Treats handedness like SOC 2: attestations, audits, remediation plans. Straight-faced about absurd fixes (digital text cannot smudge; we fixed it anyway). Never winks. Prices stated with total confidence. Right-handed people are referred to as 'guests.'

hero.h1: Your workspace has been right-handed this whole time.
hero.sub: Sinistra is workplace messaging built mirror-first. Channels on the right. Send button on the left. Ninety percent of your company will hate it. They'll adjust, the way you did.

features.title: Mirror-First Architecture
features.blurb: The entire interface renders right-to-left. The channel rail sits on the right edge, where your dominant hand already is. Right-handed colleagues describe the layout as 'disorienting and hostile.' We log each report as a feature confirmation.

features.title: Smudge-Safe Threads
features.blurb: Threads scroll upward so your palm never drags across an unread message. Digital text cannot smudge. We fixed it anyway, and we billed for it.

features.title: Handedness SSO
features.blurb: Webcam pen-grip attestation at every login. Verified lefties get the badge. Right-handed users may join as read-only guests, pending a dexterity review they will not pass.

pricing_tiers.name: Scissors
pricing_tiers.tagline: Technically usable, like everything else you've been given.

pricing_tiers.name: Fountain Pen
pricing_tiers.tagline: For teams ready to stop smudging.
perks: Priority support, answered left to right

pricing_tiers.name: Full Sinistral
perks: We speak to your IT department so you never have to

testimonials.quote: I onboarded all forty people on my team. The right-handed ones quit within the week. Retention has never been easier to read.
testimonials.name: Dana Okafor
testimonials.title: VP of Internal Tooling, regional escrow company

testimonials.quote: The send button is finally where my thumb already was. I closed my laptop and sat quietly for a while.
testimonials.name: Marcus Lindqvist
testimonials.title: Staff Engineer (left-handed, verified), Series B pallet-logistics startup

testimonials.quote: We failed our first dexterity audit. Sinistra walked us through remediation. We failed the second one with much more confidence.
testimonials.name: Priya Raghunathan
testimonials.title: Director of Workplace Symmetry, 400-person actuarial firm

demo_screen_concept: The main Sinistra workspace, fully mirrored: channel rail pinned to the right edge (#left-general, #scissors-grievances, #desk-reorientation), right-aligned messages with verified-left badges, a composer whose send button sits on the far left, a handedness attestation banner, and a 'guest mode' toggle that flips the entire UI left-to-right for right-handed visitors behind a condescending confirmation dialog.

design_direction.style: Mirrored neo-brutalism. A cool paper-white ground with hard 2px ink-navy borders, flat cobalt panels, and offset solid shadows that fall down-and-to-the-LEFT, the mirror image of every other landing page on the internet. The whole composition is right-aligned: brand lockup on the right, nav links trailing leftward, headlines flush right and ragged left, section numbers in the right margin. One signal red is reserved for the primary CTA and right-handed warnings. Archivo in heavy weights for display, Public Sans for body, IBM Plex Mono for badges, prices, and audit labels. No gradients, no blur, corners squared off at 2px or less. Reads like a compliance vendor that has chosen violence in the most organized possible way.
design_direction.archetype: Enterprise collaboration SaaS landing, fully mirrored right-to-left: right-aligned hero with a flipped workspace vignette, three feature panels with left-falling shadows, three testimonials, a three-tier pricing rack, and a demo handoff to the mirrored workspace.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Hard snaps, no soft easing: hover lifts panels up-and-right while the left-falling shadow grows; press collapses the shadow flat. Entrances slide in from the right at 150ms with sharp cubic-bezier. No parallax, no floating, nothing drifts.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Brand lockup top-right, nav reading right-to-left. Oversized Archivo headline set flush right. A small bordered notice chip above it: 'This page is left-handed. The discomfort you feel is the product.' Primary CTA in signal red at the hero's bottom-right; a mirrored workspace vignette tilted slightly, shadow falling left.
